zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Conyers於1376年出生在英國達勒姆時，她的父親Robert Conyers1歲，母親Alliana Cotam26歲。她於1395年與Robert Bowes在英國英格蘭達勒姆達勒姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1438年11月7日在她的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國達勒姆包維斯。

nameMeanings.name: Conyers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire, of Norman origin): habitational name from either of two places in northern France, Coignières in Seine-et-Oise or Cogners in Sarthe. Alternatively, a variant of Conyer , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Conyer
meanings: English: occupational name for a minter, a coiner of money, from an agent derivative of Old French coignier ‘to stamp money, to mint’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Conyers was born in 1376, in Durham, England, her father, Robert Conyers, was 1 and her mother, Alliana Cotam, was 26. She married Robert Bowes in 1395, in Durham, Durham, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 7 November 1438, in her hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Bowes, Durham, England.

Elle a épousé Robert Bowes en 1395, à Durham, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. ko.lifeSummary: Jane Conyers(이)가 1376년 영국 뒤람에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Conyers(은)는 1세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alliana Cotam(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1395년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 뒤람에서 Robert Bowes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1438년 11월 7일 그녀의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 뒤람 보우스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Conyers родилась в 1376 г. в местности дурам, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Conyers было 1 год, а матери, которую звали Alliana Cotam, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Bowes в 1395 г. в местности дурам, дурам, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 7 ноября 1438 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности боуесь, дурам, Англия.
